tokens: Pitch...If I can get it over to read it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll give you an update when you get done.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Go ahead and park on your 045.
We'll give you an update when you are done.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, is it worth coming right there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like a pretty good location.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can sample the rim materials of this crater.

(Pause) Bob, I'm at, let's say, the east-southeast rim of a, oh, 30-meter crater - in the light mantle, of course - up on the Scarp and maybe 300...(correcting himself) 200 meters from the rim of Lara in a northeast direction.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It probably shows up as a bright crater on your map.
There's only about a half a centimeter of gray cover over very white material that forms the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gene, give me a call when you get parked and I'll give you an update on what we want to do.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'll take the Rover readout first.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The rear motors are off scale low and the forward motors are 0 and 240.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Understand that 240 now instead of a 340.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: If I ever gave you a motor temperature of 340, that figure was erroneous.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And what we'd like you to do, Gene, is we'd like you to get the CSVC by yourself, that will essentially be your sole task at this station.
We'd like Jack to do some solo sampling.
We'd like to get one pan and the gravimeter, and then we'll leave this station.
We're going to absorb some of the time we spent with the extra gravimeter reading and some of the time we absorbed at Station 2 in the longer stay time in sampling at Station 3.
So it will be CDR for the CSVC - or the long can, excuse me - and LMP for solo sampling and then a pan by Jack, I presume, and then the gravimeter and then leave.
And, Jack, you might check your film.
We aren't quite sure where you are right now, before you get too far from the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, you got (pause) any preference up in this area where you want that long can?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's something that was sort of near the Scarp, but you're parked so near the Scarp and that's something, remember when we do it in solo, we only did it with the Rover, so you'd have to stay right there beside the Rover and do it.
No expectations of doing it otherwise.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think you're in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I don't have any other choice.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Matter of fact, if there is a scarp, and if it is a fault...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...you're right on it because the projection of it would be uphill a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'll be right on the side of it.
I'm parked on the side of it if it exists.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and, Jack, what's your frame count?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I've dug a trench in the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Jack) You can work the rest (of the Station) without a mag change.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) (Realizing whatever it is that Gene needs) Oh, yeah.
(To Bob) Bob, I dug a trench in the side of this crater.
I've got down-Sun pictures of it.
There's quite a marbling of light and dark soil or fine grain material.
It looks as if there's a uniform, about 3-centimeter (thick) layer of light material over that marbled light and dark.
On the very top surface, there's a half centimeter of light gray, and when I say "dark", I mean a "medium gray".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Sounds like a great sample site.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'm going to start sampling the soils, and then I'll get you the fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I presume that we'll at least have the single upper core which we can use to sample that stuff in the soil.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, there's no guarantee (that we can reach it).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gene, are you still near the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we'd like to get the SEP blankets opened, Gene, and dusted if they're dirty, so they can cool some more.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now I can't give you the gravimeter reading while I'm working on the Rover, so I'll have to time it when I get away from it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you'll be pounding on the hammer for a long while while you can take the gravimeter reading.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me get your brush back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, bag 520 has a skim sample of the upper light-gray soil.
Don't know where I'm going to put these things, I've got to come down and get a bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I can't punch it until I get out of here.
(Long Pause) These (SEP) switches are in Off and Standby, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that's where they are and the temperature is about 104 and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, it doesn't matter whether it's Standby; it won't be (garbled) that temperature anyway, but put it Off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It might have gotten hit when I changed the blanket.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'm just inquiring of Bob (Overmyer, the Command Module CapCom).
He's just passed a little bit north of you a couple of minutes ago and took some pictures of you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I do my work around the LMP seat here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, the upper...The upper 5 centimeters...(adjusting his estimate as he looks at the layer) 3 centimeters mixed with that upper half centimeter, is the next sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I guess I'm going to go pound away (on a drive tube); and, Jack, I'm going to hit the gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, the first core has gone down pretty good, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, you won't have any problem in here coring.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, man, I tell you, I wish I was putting a drill hole in here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The next sample is mostly the medium-gray fraction of the marbling.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I think I got it.
I think I got it, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And, Jack, when you get done with this trench you might hit one or two of those blocks there, but then we'd...Since we're really trying to cut this station down to a minimum, after that you'd probably better get to the pan.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, what do you think, can I read a gravimeter?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, it should be just done.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, the white fraction in the marble zone (is) in 523.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I forgot to give you the core numbers, but I will.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And don't forget to put your little note in the long can there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, I'll get the note in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, 524 is what I think is a blue-gray rock, probably the breccia.
It got a little dust covered.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: From just off the rim of this little crater.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's a blue-gray rock, it's not part of the trench, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: If you see it, Bob, it's full.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We see a long thing in your hand there, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I didn't think that was supposed to happen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Thought I had them (the core tube caps) on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, you got some there in that little (pause) pocket.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, and there's so many bags in here, I can't get at them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I mean the pocket on the Rover, on the back.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I took them out (of SCB-8) and put them on you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The rest of them are in this bag (SCB-7).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See, there's others, but I don't want to get under your seat.
We got those bags packed in there like gangbusters.
How are you doing there by yourself?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your hook (attachment for the SCB on the PLSS) came off.
If you wait a minute, I'll hook it on this bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I didn't think the sample bags could come off the camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Might) just as well fix this bag now.
It's going to come off at the bottom if I don't.
It's going to come off again.
I don't think the harness is tight enough now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I might just as well do it, so it's right.
(A little exasperated) If it's worth doing at all, it's worth doing right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, let me try getting that bag back on.
No, don't bend over; I can't get down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This bag, that hook...There's something changing the geometry.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, don't worry about it too much, guys; I'm sure the bag will stay on without the hook.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Garbled under Bob) (Responding to Bob) Yeah, it will; (that's) the conclusion I just came to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, what I know is a blue-gray breccia is in bag 525.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) And, Jack, (are) you just scooping up little rocks along the surface there...in your little xenolith mode?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) Yeah, you read my mind.
I do want to get one of these light-colored rocks, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, when I broke the cores apart, there's just a lot of dry clods; and the bottom core's full, the top core about - oh, I got to look - it's dark down there, but about an inch (to an) inch and a half of the core has just zero-g'd or one-sixth-g'd itself right out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess we still just cover it, and see what we got.
Might just again trying compacting it after that's through...after you're done with the lower core.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Geno, how about some core numbers.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, into the long can...I'll give it (the core tube number) to you; wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That may have been a piece of gabbro.
But again, I can't be completely sure.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's either that or anorthositic gabbro we saw up on the front.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And my bags aren't staying on my camera worth a darn.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Forty-six, Bob, is gone into the long can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the LMP has gone to Intermediate (cooling).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, by the way, I'm at about 49 percent (oxygen remaining) and 3.85 (psi suit pressure) and Intermediate cooling and no flags.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Have you got a number for the upper core when you're done?
I guess you're probably putting the other one (the lower) in the long can, aren't you, right now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And somewhere here along the line, Jack, I guess maybe when you get those, you ought to stop and take the pan.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, the long can is sealed and I guess nobody knows what's in it but me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I may not even tell.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It does not...None of the material in that core - in either the top section or the bottom section - looks unlike that stuff just beneath the surface that we sampled at that special (Scarp gravimeter) stop back there.
It's a bluish-gray, and it tends to clod and break up in your hands.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, you've got better than...Oh, you've got two-thirds of a(n upper) core after I packed it down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that little set of four samples is in 527, barely.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we hope it was worth the effort.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, it's all worth the effort; it just hurts (the hands).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're ready now for your pan and don't forget your scoop.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You don't mind a little dirt here and there, do you, gang?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Gene, would you go over and help Twinkletoes, please?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I tell you, you fix that camera bracket so the bags stay on and I'll be a lot better off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, you might worry about whether your camera lens is dirty or clean, Jack.
I don't know what you'd do about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I don't know what you'd do about it, but you might worry about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I don't have a thing to do...(Pause) It's clean.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll be a son of a gun!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Chuckles) I can't get this thing (the rake) locked on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I turn that like that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, if you haven't started your pan, could we get an EMU check from you?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Get back in there (into the stowage bracket on the gate).
That (rammer) goes back on your back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you go over towards Jack, Gene, and then the two of you can pick up the scoop and the bag together and get back towards the Rover after that?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I'm cleaning up his seat here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I can hack it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And then, at that point, we're ready for you guys to leave.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Jack, I've got the rammer I've got to put on you.
I'll just leave it on your seat right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I said we're watching you, but don't let that inhibit you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I don't let anything inhibit me.
And I don't stay mad very long.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there's an easy way to do everything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The question is can you hang on to it once you've done it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Facing away from Gene) Where are you?
They don't stay on my camera anymore.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's no reason why they shouldn't, according to this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Garbled) the samples from that...Oh...I need it...I gotta go up there.
Cross-Sun, from over to the north of the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I got it; I'll get it.
If you'll mount this thing (the SCB Jack was using).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, what else do you want us to do here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Get on the Rover and leave.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're going back to get that "after", and we won't forget it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you might be able to decipher this station, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And be advised that the switchboard here at MSC (Manned Spacecraft Center, now the Johnson Space Center) has been lit up by calls from the Houston Ballet Foundation requesting your services for next season.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we can't use that one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Asking Bob about his ballet style) How's that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...and the left one is almost non-functional.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Once you get it (the bag latch on the gate) open, you can't get it locked.
I'll dust them if I get a chance, but it's locked on the left side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, well, we'll keep those bags underneath the seat, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think the samples are safer under there, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we can take bag 7 out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me put the rammer on your back and see if we can't get this (sample bag holder) on your camera.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Are we going to run the SEP this time?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know; he hasn't said anything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, we will not turn the SEP on, guys.
You might cover it with the (thermal) blankets as well as you can.
And how about a temperature reading before you leave, when you do that?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: 100, and understand both switches are Off and the covers are closed.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, the covers are closed now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They're not going to stay closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I don't know why it (the bag holder on his camera bracket) isn't staying on, but it certainly isn't.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I just bent that, didn't I?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, that's not going to stay on.
Yeah, you bent it (chuckles) very well.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) I guess I bent my camera brack(et)...mounting point...the camera point.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We may have to think about a fix there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We might be able to fix that in the cockpit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, are we all through, have you got...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...(we'll) worry about that when you get back in (the LM).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, I guess I need to get another film mag, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, how about frame counts on both you guys before you start?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We suggest magaline...(correcting himself) magazine Juliett, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Laughing and indulging in some word play) Okay, we'll get "magaline" Julieing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know, I'd enjoy this if it weren't so much fun.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, you going to change your mag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (I'll) shoot a 500 while you're doing that.
(Jack laughs) No sense in me sucking my thumb.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) Any time you want to do something, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...we'd like to press on as quickly as possible.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Take a portion of the Scarp over there you can see.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We are, Bob, but he's got to change his mag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to stand here and look around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm picking up with (500 mm) mag - or (correcting himself) with frame 56 - and I'm going to try to get a little bit of where the Scarp overlaps the North Massif.
I can't see much of it...(Pause) All I could get was three frames of that.
Now I'm picking up the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, how are you coming, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I ought to put that (used mag) in there so you've got room for your (500 mm) camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got a final frame count there, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: When I finished the South Massif, I was on 94 and now I'm on 99.
I took five more pictures back over to the northeast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And we assume you guys are ready to go by now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Bob, they were all (taken) with the lens cap off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, what's the heading say?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Why don't you fix that high-gain so you can see?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll get the reading we need for the Nav update.
Do you think you can give us the heading right now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, heading is four (pause) one (41)...I think.
I'm at a little bit of an angle.
Better let Gene do it for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we were wondering if it drifted while you were there, because we're going to give you, now, a...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...going to give you one to update it, if it has drifted at all.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, it did drift, 041 is a good number.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, the hammer (caught again under the console)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We didn't really do all the things we wanted to do, but I think we did everything we could.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We did everything we wanted to (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, let's get ready to roll.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I thought I handed...Didn't you stick it in?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I sure thought I handed it to you, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You did, and I put it in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we've recommendations for Minimum (cooling) for you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think I am in Minimum.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And give us a mark rolling, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, Bob, I gave you one.
We've been rolling for about 30 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're at 087 and 5.9 on the range.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the drive to Station 4 will be nominal and we'll get a Rover sample at about 094/5.1 but it will be the track as indicated on the map and the cuff checklist.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I got it in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I know we're next to that bend (in the traverse path indicated on the checklist map), but I know where we're going.
My next is 094/5.1 is what I want for that sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Five point one; zero nine four, five decimal one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Realizing his mistake) Oh, okay, that's the heading.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) You going to drive by this big rock?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see when that (reflection off the) LCRU shines into my eyes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like one of the (blue) gray breccias.
(To Bob) Big 3- to 4-meter block out here all by itself on the light mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it looked like a gray breccia.
All I could see was the surface texture, and it had the nodular or elongate nodular texture that those breccias had up on the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the battery temperatures are 100 and 130.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, as far as any of the things we talked about trying to see at the surface - dynamics or a variation of the light mantle - I think you've heard it all.
There isn't much to say about the dynamics right now.
I have a feeling that the surfaces are old enough that all those kind of detailed relationships have been obscured.
Filleting is just about the same all over here.
It varies, but there are no systematics that I've seen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do we ever see a Rover flag come up when you've got high temperatures there on the battery.
Have you seen the flag up yet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, you did not; you did not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that may be telling us something, we hope (about a gauge problem rather than a battery problem).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (I) ought to cut left up here a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What was the aspect ratio (depth divided by width) of that little thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's what they call a pit crater.
Hey, can you swing a little bit and let me get that fragment (rimmed) crater?
See that one on your left there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Garbled) crater we've seen up here (on the light mantle).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're at 090/5.3 for a quick Rover sample of a very, very fragmental crater.
The ejecta is about fifty percent small, angular fragments; much different than we have seen before in terms of the type of patterns.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and that's in bag 41 Yankee.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Very pleased with their performance) About a 30-second stop!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I couldn't tell whether that was just...It looked like that might have been a crater that had got to bedrock.
There may have been a high point, or let's say a thin point, in the light mantle, and it got down to bedrock.
But I can't...It's the most blocky-rimmed crater we've seen for a long time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: All these others are nowhere near that...Look at that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It was about 15 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, there are no obvious lineations - at the scale we can observe - on the light mantle.
I think the pan photography and the metric stuff (cameras in the SIM Bay of the CSM) may be what you'll have to use for any directional trends out in here.
Depending on what we decide the origin (of the light mantle) is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Making a mis-identification) We're still reading you, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Are you reading us through the LM or through the low-gain?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As far as I can tell, we're reading you through the low-gain.
It's been working just great tonight, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (In a tone of mock outrage) Tonight?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...because I just scraped bottom on the LCRU.
If it's still working, I'm glad to hear that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Going to be right on the rim of that crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, 17, the word from the Backroom is: with that last Rover sample you got, we'd like to go straight to Station 4.
We won't get the one here at 094 and 5.3...(Correcting himself) 5.1, excuse me.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I thought the purpose was to sample the light mantle?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We talked to them about that, but they...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We didn't sample light mantle at that last one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I talked to them about that.
But they are so anxious to get to Station 4, I guess they don't want to do it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think we got to, right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And give me a bearing and a range to (means "at") Station 4.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It will be bearing of 100 and range of 4.6.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Was that one-zero-zero, four point six?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, there aren't very many rocks that just sit on the surface.
All of them seem to be slightly buried to moderately buried.
(Pause) That one looked like it might be vesicular.
There's a trench...(a) linear set of craters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We got to do a little detouring.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, what we're looking for is 101...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just get down this slope.
I don't see Shorty though, do you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wait a minute, is that it?
Is that it out there straight ahead?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let me get down this slope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It might be right over there to the left a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I can cut it right across there.
That's going to be about the right place.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Seems a little far from here, but...Maybe not.
(Pause) Oh, I forgot to take pictures again.
(Pause) That Scarp certainly is spectacular going up there by Hanover, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It just rolls over the side, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I don't know what else we can say about it, though.
(Chuckles) Okay, we're getting a good view of the North Massif, and the cross-hatched lineaments that Gene has talked about are over there, also.
They seem to be a set that plunge about, oh, 30 degrees to the east and another set that plunge about the same to the west.
Plus the boulder tracks, which we see occasionally over there.
And there are areas of boulder fields up on the Massif itself, such as we saw on the South Massif.
As a matter of fact, it looks like there's one just above where Station 6 may be.
Straight ahead of us there, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: About bearing 060 from our present position, which is 098 and 4.8.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I change that heading, that (reflection off the) LCRU comes right in on me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't see anything like layering up there.
Although the upper boundary of those boulder fields on the North Massif, and, as a matter of fact, on the South Massif...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's Shorty straight ahead of us, I think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yep, that's got to be it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...(continuing the interrupted thought) all tend to have a linear boundary.
That's the upper portion of the field; the lower portion is strung out downslope.
(Pause) That looks like it might be Shorty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Sounds like you're just about there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I think we got it in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, looking at the Sculptured Hills (at the east end of the valley), I think Gene's comments the other day about Bear Mountain would apply.
There's a small relief - or small amplitude hummockiness - to the surface.

It's formed by a cross-hatch of...Let's say the slope I'm looking at is sort of (a) west-facing slope on the other side of Wessex Cleft (a vale between the North Massif and the Sculptured Hills), (and) it's formed by lineaments plunging about 10 degrees to the north and about 10 degrees to the south.
And the combination gives some hummocks that are quite distinct.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, you know it's hard to see a(n ejecta) blanket here, but that's got to be Shorty right there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We want to park...I don't think we'll see a blanket...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, at least we're going to see where the break in slope is for the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, look at the boulder sitting on that rim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let us know when you stop and where you're...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I think we ought to park over here near that big boulder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, if I can get up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can swing in, you know, and just park parallel to the...Of course, that will put them looking back.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, yeah, but 045 gives me a good...Let me work on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get up there slowly.
I'll put them on this low saddle here.
045 will give them a good heading.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Shorty is a crater, the size of which you know (about 100 meters in diameter.) It's obviously darker rimmed, although the fragment population for most of the blanket does not seem too different than the light mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, are you going to get a picture now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's about as far as I want to take it!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And when you stop and get off, give me (the) word and I'll read you up some revised plans for Station 4.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, we're heading 041; bearing is 102; distance, (1)5.1; and 4.2 on the range.
Amp-hours are 92 (and) 90; 102 (degrees) and 128 on the batteries.
Off-scale low on the fronts; and off-scale low on the rears
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did I understand 4.2 on the range, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I don't know whether you're wrong, or we are (about the LM location), but...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sounds like an interesting crater, in any case.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Parker) Wait until you see the bottom of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
